title: ShipResult
description: Output of `bin/ship.sh`. Combines PR creation (via bin/pr.sh) with check-wait + merge so the user gets one invocation instead of three. Two modes: server-side `auto-merge` (default — fast return; GitHub merges when required checks + reviews pass) and `client-side` (poll-and-merge — terminal blocks until ship-or-fail, useful when auto-merge isn't enabled on the repo).

target.description: Repo path the ship was initiated from.

mode.description: auto-merge = `gh pr merge --auto` enabled server-side, ship returns immediately. client-side = ship polls wait-for-pr-checks then merges.

outcome.description: shipped = client-side merged successfully. queued = auto-merge enabled, GitHub will merge when checks pass. ci-failed / ci-timeout = client-side waiter bailed. merge-failed = client-side merge step failed (e.g. branch protection blocked). pr-failed = PR creation step failed. skipped = gh missing/unauthenticated, no PR was created.

elapsed_seconds.description: Wall-clock seconds the ship took, including any client-side polling. 0 in the skipped path.

pr_url.description: URL of the created PR. Absent only when `outcome` is `skipped` or `pr-failed`.

pr_number.description: PR number. Absent under the same conditions as `pr_url`.

merge_strategy.description: Merge strategy used (auto-merge or client-side). From --merge-strategy or profile.github.auto_merge_strategy. Default squash.

checks.description: Present only in `client-side` mode. Mirrors the summary block of the wrapped wait-for-pr-checks call.

merge_failed_reason.description: Set when `outcome == "merge-failed"`. Capped error text from `gh pr merge`.

ci_failed_reason.description: Set when `outcome == "ci-failed"` for a structured reason a generic CI failure can't carry — currently only the no-checks-attached case in client-side mode (workflows hadn't registered yet, or the repo runs no PR CI; re-run with --allow-no-checks if intentional). Absent for unstructured CI failures.
